Ad Text:
As a Dynatrace Consultant, you will help customers optimise their IT environments and digital experiences by delivering high-quality observability and monitoring solutions using the Dynatrace platform. You will work on a variety of client-facing projects, including new implementations, environment optimisation, integrations, and ongoing enhancements.
  
This hands-on role focuses on designing, configuring, and delivering Dynatrace solutions that drive measurable business and IT outcomes. You’ll collaborate with other skilled consultants, architects, and industry experts to deliver exceptional customer experiences and successful outcomes.
  
The right candidate will also have the opportunity to work closely with the sales, pre-sales teams to identify and progress customer opportunities.  This role can be based in Sydney, Brisbane or Melbourne.

What's on offer: 

Work with like-minded consultants and architects to deliver innovative solutions across multiple, diverse industries  
An excellent remuneration package commensurate with the level of experience and contribution to the growth of the company
The opportunity to work with some of Australia's largest enterprises on complex projects leveraging Dynatrace Observability Platform to deliver business outcomes
Structured career advancement opportunities within DXC’s growing Observability practice.
Structured internal career advancement opportunities
Be part of a specialised product focus group, with opportunities to work alongside DXC Microsoft/SAP/Oracle/Salesforce delivery teams and leveraging DXC Global ServiceNow business groups
Be part of a supportive team which supports your career development and success through highly collaborative breakout sessions allowing you to enhance your skills and share knowledge
Part of team that encourages and incentivises out of the box thinking, innovation to drive products and services within the Dynatrace ecosystem
Exposure and opportunity to work in digital and business transformation
  
What you will need to succeed: 
 

5+ years’ experience in IT performance monitoring, observability, or APM, including at least 2+ years working with Dynatrace.
Proven ability to design and implement Dynatrace monitoring solutions across applications, infrastructure, and cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GCP).
Hands-on experience with Dynatrace OneAgent, dashboards, Davis AI, and problem analysis.
Knowledge of integrations and automation, including linking Dynatrace with ITSM tools (e.g., ServiceNow), CI/CD pipelines, and cloud services.
Strong skills in troubleshooting application and infrastructure performance issues, including root cause analysis.
Experience working with stakeholders to gather requirements, deliver workshops, and present actionable insights to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team in fast-paced, client-facing environments.
Relevant certifications (e.g., Dynatrace Associate or Professional) are highly desirable.
Excellent communication, time management, and problem-solving skills.
Understanding of agile project delivery methodologies.

The Opportunity
We are working with a Sydney-based, private equity-owned asset seeking an Interim Transformation and Integration specialist for a high-impact, 6-month engagement. Operating at the intersection of transformation and M&A integration, this role exists to establish a fit-for-purpose operating model for a business navigating simultaneous cost transformation and post-acquisition change across systems, people, and processes.
 
The Role
This is an interim opportunity for a seasoned program and transformation professional, with a commercial mindset and operational understanding. You will need to quickly diagnose the landscape, identify the priorities, and build the structure around which sustained outcomes can be delivered. This role requires someone who can navigate ambiguity and be hands on, while also having the confidence to navigate stakeholders at all levels including the Board, Exec team and PE partners.
 
Key Responsibilities:

Identify and prioritise material, actionable cost reduction opportunities with quantified savings targets
Design and mobilise a transformation roadmap with clear milestones and outcomes
Design, implement and embed a fit-for-purpose operating model, translating strategy into tangible structural change
Project manage post-acquisition integrations across systems, people, and processes, ensuring clean, controlled, and compliant execution
Drive structural cost reset including role simplification, automation and centralisation where applicable
Track and validate benefits realisation, ensuring savings are reflected in run rate
Own stakeholder relationships at all levels, taking clear accountability for outcomes and maintaining momentum with the Executive team, Board, and PE partners
Prepare a clean handover to BAU or permanent leadership, leaving the organisation in a stronger, simpler position
 What You'll Bring:

Proven transformation and integration experience, with a demonstrable track record of delivering outcomes in compressed timeframes
Pre/post-acquisition integration and M&A experience across systems, people, and processes is essential
Strong programme management capability, with experience owning complex, multi-workstream delivery end-to-end
Experience designing and implementing operational models and process optimisation and automation as part of broader structural change
Highly developed commercial acumen with the ability to identify and cost efficiencies and high-value opportunities quickly
Confident operating across all levels, from frontline teams through to senior executives, Boards, and PE partners
Hands-on execution style, with the ability to navigate ambiguity and drive decisions to create impact  
Experience with private equity owned assets preferred  
To be eligible for this role, you must be able to commit to at least 6 months, be available immediately or on short notice, and have full Australian work rights, without restrictions for the duration of the contract.

data management

Ad Text:
Senior Business Analyst – Innovation Stream

Employment Type: Full Time (35 hours per week)
Duration: 8 Month Contract
Remuneration: Level 9 $148,012 - $157,056 (depending on experience) + 17% super + leave loading
Location:  UNSW Kensington Campus, with fortnightly day trips to Canberra (schedule may vary).

The Opportunity

UNSW is establishing a new strategic presence in the nation’s capital through the Canberra City Program - a major growth initiative designed to expand UNSW’s education, research, defence and innovation footprint in Canberra.

We are seeking an experienced Senior Business Analyst with a strong background in translating growth and commercial strategy into operationally executable models. Reporting to the Enterprise Portfolio Management Office (EPMO), you will be embedded in the Innovation stream, playing a key role in shaping how UNSW enables innovation, scales partnerships, and operationalises its strategic ambitions.

This role is ideal for a Business Analyst who operates comfortably at the intersection of strategy, commercial intent and delivery, and who enjoys turning complex, high-level objectives into clear operating models, services and ways of working.

About the Program & Innovation Stream

The Canberra City Program spans five strategic streams: Education, Innovation, Operating Model, Estate, and Site Technology, leveraging UNSW’s strong defence and industry partnerships to drive sustainable growth.

Within the Innovation stream, the focus is on:

Enabling innovation at scale
Designing commercially viable partner engagement models
Establishing repeatable, scalable approaches to onboarding and supporting partners
Ensuring strategic growth objectives are supported by fit-for-purpose processes, services and governance

Key Responsibilities

In this role, you will:

Partner with senior stakeholders to translate growth, innovation and commercial strategies into practical operating models
Analyse strategic objectives and commercial drivers to define how innovation initiatives and partnerships should operate in practice
Lead the design and documentation of end-to-end innovation and partner journeys, from initial engagement through onboarding and ongoing delivery
Facilitate workshops to explore strategic intent, test assumptions, identify constraints, and align stakeholders on future-state models
Identify gaps between current-state capability and future growth ambitions, and develop clear, implementable improvement options
Develop service models and service catalogues that clearly define ownership, roles and accountabilities across UNSW and external partners
Translate qualitative insights and strategic priorities into clear business requirements, process models and procedural documentation
Support continuous improvement, standardisation and scalability across innovation-related processes and services
Work closely with delivery teams to ensure strategic decisions are executed effectively and embedded sustainably

About You

You bring:

Significant experience as a Senior Business Analyst on large-scale growth, transformation or commercially focused initiatives
A strong track record of bridging strategy and execution, particularly in complex or federated organisations
Experience working with or supporting innovation ecosystems, partnerships, or externally facing programs
Advanced skills in journey mapping, operating model design, process mapping and service design
Confidence engaging with senior stakeholders to challenge assumptions and shape outcomes
The ability to turn ambiguity into structure, and strategy into clear, actionable delivery artefacts
Exceptional facilitation, analytical and written communication skills

Experience in higher education, public sector, or defence-related environments will be well regarded, but is not essential.

This role can be based in Sydney or Canberra and is open to Australian citizens only.

Why UNSW?

You’ll have the opportunity to:

Contribute to a high-profile national growth initiative
Work at the intersection of strategy, innovation and delivery
Influence how UNSW partners, innovates and scales for the future
Be part of a collaborative, purpose-driven environment with real impact

For further details on role responsibilities and skills & experience required, refer to the position description found under the position listing on Jobs@UNSW

People are at the core of everything we do. We recognise it is the contributions of our staff who make UNSW one of the best universities in Australia and the world.

Our benefits include:

Career development opportunities.

Ad Text:

Does this sound like the next step in your career? If so, Apply now!

Position Overview:


As a Senior Payroll Business Analyst, you will play a critical role in leading payroll system initiatives, process optimisation, and data-driven transformations within a complex organisational environment. With deep expertise in SAP Payroll and/or Dayforce (Ceridian), you will work closely with HR, Finance, and IT stakeholders to analyse requirements, design solutions, and ensure accurate, compliant payroll outcomes. Your leadership will drive system configurations, data migrations, integrations, and ongoing support, while maintaining strict adherence to Australian legislative requirements including Single Touch Payroll (STP), Superannuation, and tax obligations.
In this role, you will be responsible for end-to-end business analysis, solution design, testing, and stakeholder management. Your focus will be on translating complex payroll needs into effective system functionality, proactively identifying risks, and delivering high-quality outcomes that support seamless payroll processing for a large workforce.


Key Responsibilities:

Lead requirements gathering, analysis, and documentation for payroll processes, including pay rules, deductions, tax calculations, leave management, benefits, and reporting across SAP Payroll and/or Dayforce systems.

Design, configure, and optimise payroll solutions in SAP HCM Payroll and/or Dayforce Payroll modules, ensuring full compliance with Australian legislation, Superannuation Guarantee, Fringe Benefits Tax, and STP Phase 2 requirements.

Act as the primary subject-matter expert for SAP Payroll schemas, rules, functions, wage types, and payroll drivers, while leading configuration and enhancements in Dayforce for time & attendance, benefits integration, and end-to-end payroll processing.

Drive system integration, data migration, and transition projects between SAP Payroll and Dayforce environments, including detailed source-to-target mapping, transformation rules, reconciliation, and cutover support.

Collaborate with HR, Finance, and IT teams to identify payroll inefficiencies, streamline processes, and implement automation opportunities using SAP and Dayforce capabilities.

Develop and execute comprehensive test strategies, including unit, integration, parallel, and user acceptance testing (UAT) for all payroll changes and enhancements.

Department of Health | eHealth Queensland

About the role Reporting to the Project Director, the Principal Business Analyst – Cyber Security will lead business analysis activities across one or more major cyber initiatives. This role is focused on delivering high-quality requirements, process design and business analysis artefacts for large-scale IM/ICT cyber security projects.
You will manage the end-to-end analysis lifecycle, including:

business case development
requirements elicitation and validation
gap analysis
future-state modelling
review and uplift of vendor-produced artefacts
This role has a strong focus on cyber security technologies and initiatives such as Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA), Data Loss Prevention (DLP), Network Security, Security Governance, and Microsoft Entra ID. There are two contract opportunities available: one for 12 months and one for 6 months.
Key responsibilities In this role, you will:

Lead business analysis activities for large-scale cyber security projects
Analyse business requirements and system specifications to ensure alignment with business needs

Review deliverables from external vendors, identify gaps and improve document quality

About you To succeed in this role, you will bring proven experience leading end-to-end business analysis activities within large, complex IM/ICT environments, ideally in cyber security and/or healthcare.
You will demonstrate:

Strong experience leading business analysis activities within IM/ICT projects, preferably in cyber security

Highly regarded The following experience will be highly regarded:

Domain knowledge in cyber security strategy, risk management frameworks, and security architecture concepts

Experience delivering projects that establish security foundations and strengthen organisational cyber maturity

Job details


The successful candidate will:


Focus on the immediate, hands-on, tactical execution of the ERP migration, specifically the crucial process of data cleansing, quality assurance, mapping and loading. Specifically, but not limited to HR and Financial data.

Proven ability to define, document and execute data cleansing and remediation rules for large-scale migration efforts.

Experience establishing processes to securely collect, process and validate data, including the ability to define business rules and run data quality checks against them.

Strong understanding of data governance standards, policies and practices, particularly related to data quality management, consistency and integrity.

Proven ability to prepare presentations, write reports and communicate complex data quality findings and patterns to technical and non-technical stakeholders using dashboards and reports.

Hands-on experience developing dashboards and interactive visualisations, ideally using Power BI and Tableau.

Previous ERP implementation or ERP data migration experience

Experience working with ERP systems (example Aurion, TechOne, WorkDay, SAP, MS365)

Experience using the Aurion Query Tool (AQT)
Experience using the TechOne analyser ETL tool

Strong SQL skills and experience querying large enterprise datasets

Experience with Python for data analysis, transformation, or automation

Experience performing data profiling, cleansing, and validation

Experience with data reconciliation between source and target systems during migrations

Experience working with Federal Government HR or financial data

Experience producing data mapping, transformation logic, and migration templates

Experience with data quality assessment and remediation activities

Ability to work with multiple stakeholders

Experience documenting data definitions, business rules, and data lineage

Experience mentoring internal data professionals, providing targeted feedback on technical skills and improving communication of analytical insights.

Proven ability to prepare presentations, write reports and communicate complex data quality findings and patterns to technical and non-technical stakeholders using dashboards and reports.

About the role:

About Western Health

Western Health is one of the largest healthcare providers in Victoria, caring for a vibrant, diverse and rapidly growing community of one million people in Melbourne's western suburbs.

With more than 13,000 staff, Western Health delivers 800,000+ occasions of care every year, providing high-quality, person-centred care across every life stage.

We provide a combination of hospital, community-based, at-home and in-reach services to aged, adult and paediatric patients and newborn babies. Specialties include oncology, renal, mental health, women's health and maternity, chronic diseases, geriatrics and cardiology.

We have remarkable strengths in chronic disease, complex care and developing innovative and sustainable healthcare solutions. Our experience at the coalface of healthcare delivery for some of Australia's most culturally and socioeconomically diverse and vulnerable populations provides us with unique insights into the most pressing health challenges.

Western Health operates four acute public hospitals: Sunshine Hospital (including Joan Kirner Women's and Children's), Footscray Hospital, Williamstown Hospital and Bacchus Marsh Hospital. We also operate Sunbury Community Hospital, Sunshine Mental Health and Wellbeing Centre, Melton Health and Community Services, Bacchus Marsh Community Health Centre, Caroline Springs Community Health Centre and Grant Lodge Residential Aged Care in Bacchus Marsh. Western Health also provides the primary healthcare service at the Dame Phyllis Frost Centre, a women's prison located in Ravenhall.

Be the bridge between customers and delivery, turning strategy into successful outcomes!

 Join Objective’s Information Intelligence team, where we empower government and regulated industries to unlock actionable insights and drive transformational outcomes through data-driven solutions. As our Engagement Manager, you’ll be at the heart of mission-critical projects, ensuring our cloud-based software delivers maximum value and transparency to our customers and their communities.

In this role, you'll champion project delivery across Objective Products, with a focus on our fast-evolving Information Intelligence portfolio. You’ll play a pivotal part in establishing trusted partnerships, shaping long-term engagement strategies, and ensuring our solutions deliver real, measurable impact for customers who depend on us to make informed decisions.

You’ll collaborate with experts across delivery, sales, and product to create an environment where both our clients and our teams excel.

 All applicants must hold a valid NV1 Clearance. 

A day in your role

Engagement Management - Deliver projects in line with Objective’s methodology, applying strong governance and controls to ensure successful, low-risk outcomes. Leads engagements and contributes at Steering Committee level.

Customer Referenceability - Build trusted, long-term relationships with customers, driving value and ensuring sites become strong advocates of Objective’s solutions.

Project Outcomes- Ensure delivery on time, within budget, and to quality standards through disciplined execution and adherence to approval frameworks.

Stakeholder Management - Act as the primary escalation point, managing senior stakeholders and aligning customer and delivery teams. Represents projects in governance forums and executive updates.

Risk Management - Identify, manage, and mitigate risks proactively. Maintains risk registers and escalates where required to protect project success and reputation.

Internal Governance - Lead governance forums, secures approvals, manages escalations, and provides regular updates on progress, risks, and financial performance.

Leadership - Lead project teams and governance groups, setting direction, priorities, and ensuring effective delivery.

Global Practice Contribution - Drive continuous improvement through knowledge sharing, mentoring, and development of best practices, tools, and reusable assets.

Bid & Sales Support - Support business growth through proposals, shaping delivery approaches, estimating scope, and contributing to commercial strategy.

Utilisation - Maintain a personal billable utilisation target aligned with Regional Services Director expectations.

Your skills, experience and beyond:

Strong experience in project and program management, with deep expertise in governance, delivery frameworks, and implementation methodologies. Proven ability to manage timelines, resources, and budgets across multiple projects.

Solid understanding of the software development lifecycle, system architectures, and emerging technologies, with awareness of industry trends.

Strong commercial acumen, with sound knowledge of governance, risk mitigation, project financials, and pricing.

Proven ability to build and maintain trusted customer relationships, effectively manage escalations, and identify opportunities to drive growth and value.

Responsibilities


Team Introduction The Measurement team is responsible for demonstrating and improving the effectiveness of advertising on TikTok. We partner with advertisers, agencies, and cross-functional teams to interpret campaign performance, shape measurement strategies, and unlock stronger business outcomes through data-driven insights.

Our team sits at the intersection of sales, product, analytics, and marketing. We help clients understand how TikTok drives results across branding and performance objectives, while also influencing the evolution of measurement products, solutions, and partnerships. About the Role

As a Measurement Partner, you will be responsible for advancing TikTok’s measurement agenda in one of our key growth markets. You will work closely with leading advertisers and agencies across a range of verticals, supporting both branding and performance-focused businesses to better understand and improve the impact of their investments on TikTok. This role requires a strong blend of client consulting, analytical problem-solving, commercial acumen, and cross-functional leadership.

You will design measurement strategies, shape learning agendas, deliver advanced analytical projects, and help scale best-in-class measurement practices across the market. The ideal candidate is highly driven, intellectually curious, empathetic, and comfortable operating in a fast-paced, evolving environment. Success in this role requires strong execution, a consultative mindset, and the ability to turn data into actionable narratives that influence senior stakeholders and drive business growth.

Responsibilities

Drive adoption and growth of TikTok’s core measurement solutions and methodologies across your designated market.
Partner with clients and agencies to develop tailored measurement strategies and learning agendas aligned to business objectives.
Design and implement end-to-end measurement plans using 1st-party and 3rd-party solutions across branding and performance use cases.
Advise advertisers on how to use measurement to improve media effectiveness, campaign performance, and budget allocation decisions.
Lead advanced analytical projects and custom studies that provide clear proof points on TikTok’s effectiveness.
Translate complex data and measurement findings into clear, compelling recommendations for clients, agencies, and internal stakeholders.
Serve as a trusted measurement advisor to key clients, media agencies, and research vendors.
Partner closely with Sales, Client Solutions, Product Marketing, Measurement Product, Business Marketing, and other cross-functional teams to deliver coordinated, high-impact support.
Build and scale programs, frameworks, and best practices that address recurring advertiser measurement needs.
Synthesize client and agency feedback to inform future product enhancements, partnership priorities, and go-to-market strategy.
Represent the market perspective to regional and global stakeholders, helping shape measurement narratives, product roadmaps, and strategic priorities.
Contribute to regional thought leadership through case studies, market insights, and scalable measurement points of view.
Qualifications


Minimum Qualifications

Significant experience in digital advertising, marketing analytics, media measurement, market research, or a related field.
Strong understanding of advertising measurement methodologies across brand and performance use cases, including both 1st-party and 3rd-party approaches.
Proven ability to translate data and insights into strategic recommendations that influence client investment decisions and business outcomes.
Experience managing external stakeholders, including advertisers, agencies, and/or research partners.
Strong project management skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Excellent communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to simplify complex technical concepts for non-technical audiences.
Professional fluency in English.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience working in or with digital platforms, media agencies, advertisers, or measurement providers.
Strong foundation in quantitative research, experimentation, attribution, lift studies, MMM, or broader marketing effectiveness solutions.
Demonstrated success in driving measurement adoption and scaling programs in a market-facing role.
Ability to navigate ambiguity, influence cross-functional stakeholders, and operate effectively in a high-growth environment.
Familiarity with the advertising and media ecosystem is highly preferred.

Boronia Capital is a successful boutique Family Office with a track record established over 25 years. We use sophisticated mathematical techniques to trade global Futures and FX markets 24 hours a day using automated systematic models. We trade from co-located data centres in five global locations. Boronia Capital has fostered a friendly and collaborative company culture that encourages high achievement and continuous improvement.

Boronia Capital is currently looking for a Quantitative Researcher to join our Research team. The primary task will be to undertake research into the detection and exploitation of robust statistically significant patterns within financial time series data with the aim of developing automated trading strategies. The successful individual will be expected to contribute novel concepts, trading ideas and trading strategies and be adept at taking those models to proof of concept and then overseeing them to production trading.

The ideal candidate will have a Ph.D /Masters degree in one of physics, engineering, mathematics, statistics or quantitative finance. Knowledge of or past research experience in any of the fields of complex systems, statistical and numerical analysis, machine learning, pattern recognition, time-series modelling or related would also be highly regarded. Ideally the candidates will have 3-5+ years of experience in the field and have worked with Family Offices, Proprietary trading firms, Systematic hedge funds or Bank quantitative desks, and have a demonstrated record of achievement and knowledge.

It is expected that you will be able to demonstrate one or more of the following:

An advanced understanding of systematic Futures trading strategies across any or all of Commodities, Fixed Income, Equities and FX.

An advanced understanding of systematic of ETF/Stock trading strategies

Advanced understanding of methods for achieving low-impact, high-capacity, near-optimal trade execution.

Insights into market impact effects and the scaling of trading systems.

An excellent understanding of risk and approaches to risk control across a portfolio of trading systems.

Comprehensive knowledge of methods for evaluating, back testing and optimising trading strategies and portfolios of strategies.

Stronger than average programmatic experience (see below)

Candidates are expected to have the strong competency in one or more of Python, Matlab, C++ or C for backtest model implementation, and should be willing to work in a collaborative way with other researchers and professionals in the Firm.

A remuneration package including base, super and bonus will be offered according to experience and qualifications.

Emergency Management Victoria (EMV) is seeking a Principal Data Analyst, to provide a critical role within Emergency Management Technology and Data (EM T&D). 

About the Business Unit 
EMV, within the Department of Justice and Community Safety, is responsible for the overall coordination of the emergency management system in Victoria. EMV drives the integration of policy, strategy, operational coordination, and planning across Victoria's emergency management sector, contributing to safer, more resilient communities. 

EMV works closely with government and agencies across the emergency management portfolio. The organisation is led by the Deputy Secretary/ Chief Executive and Emergency Management Commissioner, EMV and includes oversight of statutory entities and offices and the department's emergency management accountabilities. 

The EMT&D division is responsible for key Emergency sector common digital platforms for the emergency services sector. EMT&D is a trusted partner to emergency sector organisations, supporting the delivery of modern and effective Digital Infrastructure services tailored to the emergency sector's needs. Its main service functions include EM Strategic Planning and Business Development, EM Enterprise Architecture, EM Service Management & Development, EM National and State Commercial management and Procurements, Program delivery, Data Analytics and Investment Planning. 

About the Role 

Some of your duties will include: 

Provide strategic advice and implementation services to the data analytics and insights function, ensuring that EM T&D has the insights needed to support recovery decisions for individuals, communities and regions affected by emergencies, and deliver on key organisational objectives. 
Provide authoritative advice on the policy and strategic implications of data analytics and insights matters, including building organisational data capability.  
Actively identify opportunities to establish and develop a strong evidence culture in EM T&D, that includes improving both demand for, and supply of, evidence to support evidence-informed decision making. 

To learn more about this role before applying, please read the attached position description. 

About you  

To be successful in this role, you will have: 

At least seven years of experience in a position working on complex data management projects, data analytics, evidence and insights (or equivalent function).  
Demonstrated experience identifying business requirements and developing analytics and insights solutions, showcasing the value of analytics to the organisation.    
Business acumen, through a demonstrated understanding of concepts such as:  
Technology Alignment - understanding how technology can be leveraged to solve business problems.  
Macro-Perspective - understanding of the customer's business strategy, current business issues and priorities and current industry trends.  
Business Knowledge - understanding of business measurement of key performance indicators and business frameworks.  
Business Commentary - articulation of insight to explain current and forecasted trends, their impact and opportunities for the business.  
Advanced proficiency in data analytics tools, ideally having worked with Snowflake and Tableau, including analytical languages such as Python.  
Machine learning and artificial intelligence experience is highly desirable. 
Experience in using Jira, Confluence and project management methodologies.  

Qualifications 

A Bachelor's degree in mathematics, economics, data science or statistics (or equivalent) is highly desirable.

Ad Text:
Represent the RBA and drive engagement with industry business leaders to help inform economic policy

Leadership of the Perth office, including coaching and developing a team of highly capable economists

9-month parental leave cover opportunity

The Role

The Regional Industry Analysis (RIA) liaison program provides the Bank with invaluable insights into the experience and challenges facing Australian businesses and provides a significant contribution to monetary policy advice and improving our understanding of the economy. The team interviews executives at around 800 Australian firms and other important organisations and also works closely with other sections in Economic Group. 

The primary purpose of the role is to lead liaisons, grow the range of external contacts to support the effectiveness of the Bank’s liaison program, lead analysis that supports monetary policy advice and lead other staff in the Perth Office to reach their potential.

Working for RIA provides opportunities to gain experiences and skills that are valuable for career development including:

Building breadth and depth in leadership, communication, and decision making

Frequently leading external meetings with a wide range of organisations, often at chief executive level, and communicating the RBA's work

Significant autonomy in driving policy-relevant work plans, and multiple opportunities for cross-section collaboration and leadership

The opportunity to work out of one of our state offices (the Bank provides relocation assistance each way and a rental allowance)

Occasional travel to other areas in Western Australia, and periodic travel back to Head Office.

About you

As this is a role with a high degree of independence, a high-performance mindset and demonstrated ability to work to remove obstacles to solve problems and seize opportunities will be highly valued.

We are seeking to speak with candidates who display:

Exceptional communication skills with the ability to build strong, trusted relationships both internally and externally

Collaborative approach across departments and teams, fostering alignment, and driving progress toward organization-wide goals

Strong critical thinking and macroeconomic analysis skills

Exemplary professional conduct in internal and external dealings is essential to the role. It is expected the Senior Representative will ensure the culture in the Office is aligned with the Bank’s RBA’s authentic and inclusive values and maintain high levels of employee engagement.

Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

Why RBA?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people. Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go. Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.

Job Title: Senior Consultant ( Digital Transformation Project Manager)
Ad Text:
Company Description


Who is Turner & Townsend?

All over the world people are using buildings, infrastructure, and assets we helped to deliver. It could be the hospital they work in, the railway they travel on every day, the fuel that powers their car or the data centre they depend on at work. For more than 75 years we’ve been helping to deliver transformational programmes across the real estate, infrastructure and natural resources sectors, making a difference to people’s lives and ensuring a return on investment for our clients and their investors.

Our purpose:

Transforming performance for a green, inclusive, and productive world.

The world is changing and we have a responsibility to support that change, helping drive it and be part of it. Through the commitment, capability and care our team brings, we build trust between clients, suppliers, governments and society. Delivering better outcomes that have a positive impact on the world around us. We work smarter to face the challenges of the future; bringing the clarity that helps teams realise their full potential across the real estate, infrastructure and natural resources sectors. It’s how we’ve made the difference for more than 75 years.

Our values:

Love a challenge: We love a challenge and we work hard to make change happen and see things through. We don’t stand still, challenging ourselves and others to do better every day. And we are trusted to do the right thing, raising standards all the time.

Stronger together: We’re stronger together by connecting people in diverse teams, so that we can all collaborate to deliver our best work. We focus on what matters and use our influence to build a better world for everyone.

Bring out the best in everyone: We bring out the best in everyone. We help each other to make the most of our potential, always learning from our experience. We treat each other with care and respect and make time to give everyone a voice.

Job Description


We’re seeking a Senior Consultant (Digital Transformation Project Manager) with primary 3-6 years’ experience to manage the delivery of complex digital and data initiatives across our client portfolio. This role blends strong delivery leadership with stakeholder engagement, requiring confidence working directly with senior clients and delivery partners to drive momentum and achieve outcomes. 

The Digital Transformation Project Manager is responsible for the planning, governance, and execution of data, digital, and system enabled initiatives, ensuring projects are delivered on time, within scope, and to agreed quality standards. Success in the role depends on the ability to translate digital strategy into pragmatic, executable delivery plans that deliver measurable business value. 

This role suits someone who thrives in a client facing consulting project environment, can operate across multiple workstreams, and manages complex digital delivery. As a team, we are passionate about connecting business objectives with practical digital solutions and delivering outcomes that matter.

Key Responsibilities 

Manage the delivery of data, reporting, and digital enablement solutions across client engagements. 

Own end‑to‑end delivery of digital and data projects, ensuring outcomes are delivered on time, on budget, and to agreed quality standards. 

Act as a trusted advisor to senior stakeholders, translating business challenges into clear, pragmatic digital delivery approaches. 

Lead client workshops and steering forums to align on scope, priorities, risks, and delivery approach. 

Provide hands‑on program and PMO leadership, including schedule, risk, cost, resource management, and reporting. 

Oversee implementation of digital delivery, PMO, and portfolio management systems to improve transparency and governance. 

Translate digital and data strategies into actionable roadmaps, delivery plans, and measurable outcomes. 

Contribute to digital diagnostics, maturity assessments, and transformation roadmaps with clear findings and recommendations. 

Support business case development, including financial modelling, scenario analysis, and investment prioritisation. 

Coordinate vendors and delivery partners, ensuring accountability, delivery quality, and alignment to client outcomes. 

Govern implementation planning across the software development lifecycle, including detailed plans, milestones, resourcing, and assurance activities. 

Define business and technical requirements, including user stories, acceptance criteria, and benefits, ensuring solutions are aligned to client outcomes and realised. 

Partner with business stakeholders to manage change impacts, user readiness, and adoption activities, supporting successful transition to new digital capabilities. 

Assess and advise on client technology ecosystems, including platforms, data and analytics, and integration architecture, ensuring fit‑for‑purpose strategic alignment. 

Leverage delivery tools and methodologies (agile, hybrid, waterfall) to plan, track, and report complex digital initiatives. 

Contribute to business development through opportunity identification, proposal development, delivery planning, and value articulation aligned to client needs. 

Qualifications


Qualifications & Experience 

Degree in Business, Information Systems, Computer/Data Science, Engineering, Economics, or related discipline 

3–6 years’ experience in consulting, digital delivery management, product management, business analyst or technology transformation roles  

Background in digital consulting, business analysis, PMO, or technology project management 

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including senior‑level reporting 

Self‑driven, adaptable, and comfortable working across ambiguity and multiple priorities with strong problem-solving skills 

Knowledge and practical application of multiple project management methodologies, including Agile, Scrum, Kanban, Waterfall, and hybrid delivery models 

Preferred but not required, working knowledge of Power BI, Tableau, SQL, or equivalent data platforms (basic/intermediate) 

Experience delivering data and digital transformation initiatives, experience within the construction or property industry is advantageous but not required. 

Additional Information


Our inspired people share our vision and mission. We provide a great place to work, where each person has the opportunity and voice to affect change.

We want our people to succeed both in work and life. To support this we promote a healthy, productive and flexible working environment that respects work-life balance. 

Turner & Townsend is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community.

Please find out more about us at www.turnerandtownsend.com/

Any unsolicited resumes/CVs submitted through our website or to Turner & Townsend personal e-mail accounts, are considered property of Turner & Townsend and are not subject to payment of agency fees. Company description

Publicis Sapient is a digital transformation partner helping established organizations get to their future, digitally enabled state, both in the way they work and the way they serve their customers. We help unlock value through a start-up mindset and modern methods, fusing strategy, consulting, and customer experience with agile engineering and problem-solving creativity. United by our core values and our purpose of helping people thrive in the brave pursuit of next, our 20,000+ people in 53 offices around the world combine experience across technology, data sciences, consulting, and customer obsession to accelerate our clients’ businesses through designing the products and services their customers truly value.


Overview

As a Senior Associate in AI & Data Strategy, you will be a core contributor to Data & AI strategy engagements, playing a hands-on role in defining AI use cases, data strategies, and transformation roadmaps for clients. You will work closely with Managers and Directors to translate business problems into data and AI driven solutions, rapidly prototyping outcomes through proof of value initiatives using Publicis Sapient AI tools and accelerators.

Key Responsibilities

 Support the delivery of AI and data strategy engagements, including discovery, opportunity assessment, and roadmap definition
Be hands-on with AI tools, platforms, and accelerators to build early proofs of value
Help frame business problems into structured hypotheses and AI-led solution options
Design and validate AI use cases, assessing feasibility, business value, risk, and responsible AI considerations
Own discrete workstreams or deliverables within engagements, with guidance from Managers and Directors
Contribute to strategy artefacts including value cases, future-state visions, operating models, and transformation roadmaps
Collaborate with engineering, product, and experience teams to translate strategy into executable outcomes
Support client workshops, stakeholder interviews, and executive-ready storytelling
Conduct analysis across data maturity, platforms, analytics, and AI capabilities, contributing to target-state architecture thinking
Support AI opportunity identification, prioritisation, and decision-making through clear trade-off articulation
Contribute to proposals, POVs, thought leadership, and internal capability building through assets, demos, and reusable accelerators

Skills & Experience

4–6 years’ experience in data, AI, analytics, product, or strategy consulting roles
Practical exposure to AI, analytics, and data platforms (cloud, ML, GenAI tools)
Ability to structure problems, analyse data, and synthesise insights into clear recommendations
Familiarity with data concepts (pipelines, governance, remediation) but not expected to code heavy ETL
Comfortable working in ambiguous problem spaces with a desire to build and test solutions
Ability to create client-facing documentation, solution walkthroughs, and presentations
Comfortable engaging with product owners, business users, and engineers
Strong communication and storytelling skills
Set Yourself Apart With

Certifications in Data Management, Data Governance, or Cloud Platforms (CDMP, CDP, AWS Certified Data Analytics, etc.).
Experience with AI/ML use cases and data-driven decision-making.
Knowledge of real-time data processing and event-driven architectures.
Background in financial services, retail, or healthcare industries.

Additional information
An inclusive workplace that promotes diversity and collaboration.
Access to ongoing learning and development opportunities.
Competitive compensation and benefits package.
Flexibility to support work-life balance.
Generous paid leave and holidays.
Wellness program and employee assistance.

We are also committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application procedures. If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, you may contact us at hiring@publicis.sapient.com

Job Title: Head, Website, Publishing and Data Visualisation Unit
Company: Australian Institute of Health and Welfare
Ad Text:
The AIHW’s APS employee census results attest to our positive and supportive workplace culture. We are an inclusive, flexible, and productive workplace where people are treated with respect and courtesy, and diverse and unique attributes are recognised and valued. Our results also show that we achieved top ten rankings in staff engagement, communication, wellbeing and SES manager scales out of 107 APS agencies.

We offer a range of benefits including:

access to flexible working arrangements to support your work/life balance
attractive remuneration packages including generous superannuation and leave provisions
challenging and fulfilling work where you can use your skills and expertise
opportunities for professional development

We are committed to creating genuine opportunities for everyone and we welcome applications from First Nations people, people with disability, LGBTIQA+, neurodiverse people and people from diverse cultural and linguistic backgrounds.

The AIHW has offices in Canberra and Sydney and offers remote work opportunities for many roles.

For more information, visit the Benefits of working for the AIHW page of our website.

The Business, Communications and Publishing Services Group manages the AIHW’s corporate functions that include, HR, facilities, finance, communications and engagement, web, publishing and data visualisation and flagship reporting.  

The Web, Publication and Data Visualisation Unit (WPDVU) is responsible for managing AIHW’s primary website, aihw.gov.au. This includes:

web development (primary site, subsites and bespoke web projects), maintenance and performance
web content and release production management
web analytics
ongoing improvements to enhance production efficiency and user experience
the design and implementation of data visualisations to support clear and engaging presentations of information and data.

WPDVU also plays a leadership role in guiding and training the Institute’s reporting and authoring teams to deliver best practice content that meets the organisation’s web standards, including accessibility, clarity, and timeliness. On average around 300 analytic reports are presented on the website in pdf, html or both each year. The AIHW has been using a decentralised model for CMS content authoring since 2020 which has allowed us to enhance our capacity for content creation beyond the web team. There are approximately 260 CMS authors across the AIHW, each with varying levels of expertise and experience in HTML web content.

As well as the main AIHW website, WPDVU provides advice on four other AIHW managed websites. The WPDVU works closely with ICT, external web developers, internal authors, internal project managers and the Strategic Communications Unit. 

The AIHW is currently developing a long-term web strategy to ensure the website continues to evolve in alignment with stakeholder and user needs. An initial project has been completed to explore a future web vision, which was tested with key external stakeholders to validate assumptions and gather feedback.  This will form the foundation of the future web strategy, guiding the development of a more responsive, accessible and user-focused digital presence.

The Business, Communications and Publishing Services Group is looking for a Unit Head to lead a multidisciplinary team of web, publishing, and data visualisation specialists. They are responsible for delivering both business-as-usual operations and strategic initiatives, including innovative web product development and implementation of the long-term web strategy. The role requires a confident communicator and manager with a strong understanding of digital publishing, data and content management systems, capable of engaging with technical specialists and non-technical stakeholders alike. 

Unit Heads make a major contribution to the formulation of strategies and objectives in line with corporate goals of the Institute and take an active role in developing and promoting the Institute’s business activity. In addition to expertise in their professional field, they need advanced managerial skills to fulfil their supervisory responsibilities.

Under broad direction you will:

Provide leadership, direction and performance management to staff in the Unit to ensure the delivery of website services through the effective management of physical, financial, technological information and human resources.
With assistance from a special project team take a leading role developing and implementing the organisation’s long-term web strategy, ensuring alignment with stakeholder expectations, user needs, and emerging digital trends.
Foster strong cross-functional relationships with ICT, Strategic Communications, external developers, and internal project teams to ensure cohesive and effective digital delivery.
Support the team to develop and review relevant AIHW policies and procedures, and prepare internal management reports as needed.
Make a major contribution to the formulation of strategies and objectives in line with corporate goals and take an active role in developing and promoting the Institute’s business activity.

For more information on this role and how to apply, please visit our careers page. Applications close at 11.30pm on Thursday, 30 April 2026.
